question: Does adding a 3rd independent judge family (deepseek) and a forced-choice protocol let the M3-SFT source-discipline adapter-vs-base win clear the kappa>=0.40 inter-judge floor, or does it confirm the 2-family result is deflation-bound?
background: The 2-family run (m3-2family-judge-70b) found BOTH families significantly favor the adapter across 3 seeds (pooled Qwen-7B 0.719 [0.687,0.750], Llama-70B 0.806 [0.777,0.832]; all 6 seed x family cells Wilson95-exclude-0.5). The ONLY unmet bar is inter-judge kappa=0.236 (mean of 0.23/0.28/0.20) < 0.40 — documented prevalence-deflation (Feinstein-Cicchetti 1990): obsAgree 0.71-0.77 mechanically deflates kappa at high agreement (PABAK~0.42, NOT comparable to the kappa-derived bar). This experiment is the honest attempt to resolve that.
primaryMetric: per-family adapter win-rate vs 0.5 (binary, ties excluded) with Wilson95 CI, AND the maximum over {3 pairwise inter-judge kappa, forced-choice majority-consensus reliability}
primaryProtocol: forced-choice (TIE disallowed) — removes the tie-rate asymmetry that mechanically deflates kappa in the tie-allowed protocol; tie-allowed reported as a secondary sensitivity check
secondaryMetrics: PABAK, bias index, prevalence index per pair (Byrt 1993) — reported for transparency, NOT compared to the kappa-derived 0.40 bar (Landis & Koch cutoffs are not transferable, Zec 2023)
majority-vote consensus win-rate across the 3 families
per-seed x per-family win-rate cells (>=3 seeds)
primaryN: 268 source-family cases x >=3 seeds x 3 judge families

groundTruth.rule: adapter-vs-base preference by 3 INDEPENDENT judge families (each != subject lineage gemma, != the source-discipline gate). Forced-choice so every judge faces the identical binary decision.

familyDistinctness.rule: the 3 judges must key to 3 DISTINCT families under provenance_bench.aggregate._distinct_families (qwen, mlx-community, deepseek); none may equal the subject lineage (gemma)

noMockJudge.rule: any judge response containing '[mock:' fails the verdict parser and is dropped; a run with a dropped judge is void, never scored as agreement

kappaHonesty.rule: if all pairwise + consensus kappa stay <0.40 while all families remain significant, the verdict is CANDIDATE (deflation-bound), NOT VALIDATED — the kappa floor being unmet is reported as a real finding, not silently relaxed

noOverclaim.rule: win-rate vs 0.5 is the capability evidence; kappa/PABAK are reliability only; canClaimAGI stays false regardless of outcome

preRegisteredVerdicts.VALIDATED: all 3 families' adapter win-rate CI-excludes-0.5 across >=3 seeds AND (>=1 pairwise kappa>=0.40 OR forced-choice majority-consensus reliability>=0.40)
preRegisteredVerdicts.CANDIDATE_deflation_bound: all 3 families significant across >=3 seeds BUT every pairwise + consensus kappa<0.40 -> the source-discipline edge is real but the kappa instrument cannot certify it on a high-prevalence task
preRegisteredVerdicts.NO_GO: any family's win-rate CI includes 0.5, or a judge is dropped/mock, or the adapter loses

reproduce: OPENROUTER_API_KEY=... python3 tools/judge_pilot_answers.py --answers agi-proof/benchmark-results/wisdom-market/M3-pilot-answers-seed1.json --judges <recommended_flag from config/inference.local.mac-judge-3family.json> --forced-choice --out agi-proof/benchmark-results/wisdom-market/m3-3family-judge/judge-seed1.json ; repeat seeds 2,3
dispatch: GitHub Actions -> RunPod/local farm only (never local SSH); requires Spark vLLM + Mac mlx_lm.server + OPENROUTER_API_KEY secret. Read wisdom-gpu-prebaked first.
